subject: Sports
title: How Altitude Training Works
description: A quick win: understand How Altitude Training Works.

hook.text: Kenyan and Ethiopian runners dominate distance events. Their secret weapon is thin mountain air.

buildup.text: At high altitude, the air has less oxygen. The body compensates by producing more red blood cells.

discovery.text: More red blood cells means more oxygen delivery to muscles—a legal performance boost when competing at sea level.

twist.text: The 'live high, train low' method maximizes adaptation: sleep at altitude, train with full oxygen below.

climax.text: Kenya's Iten and Ethiopia's Bekoji sit above 7,000 feet—entire towns of elite runners shaped by altitude.

punchline.text: Thin air builds thick blood. Altitude is nature's EPO.

quiz.question: How does altitude training improve endurance?
options: The body produces more red blood cells to compensate for less oxygen
Muscles grow larger at high altitude
Gravity is weaker at altitude, making running easier
